sites.name: Cameroon Reunification Monument
sites.description: A historic monument commemorating Cameroon's 1961 reunification, featuring a spiral walkway and panoramic views of Yaoundé.

sites.name: Mvog-Betsi Zoological and Botanical Garden
sites.description: A relaxing zoo and botanical garden home to a diverse range of animal species and plants, popular with families.

sites.name: Mefou National Park (Ape Action Africa)
sites.description: A wildlife sanctuary near Yaoundé dedicated to rescuing and protecting gorillas, chimpanzees, and other primates.

sites.name: Basilica of Mary Queen of the Apostles
sites.description: A striking Catholic basilica in Mvolyé known for its architecture and panoramic hilltop views of the city.

sites.name: Blackitude Museum
sites.description: A small but rich museum showcasing traditional Cameroonian art, masks, sculptures, and ceremonial artifacts.

sites.name: Marché Central Yaoundé
sites.description: The city's largest and most popular market, offering crafts, fabrics, food, and everyday goods in a lively atmosphere.

sites.name: Chutes de Nachtigal
sites.description: Scenic rapids and falls on the Sanaga River, a popular day-trip destination surrounded by nature just outside the city.

sites.name: Lac Municipal de Yaoundé
sites.description: A municipal lake in the city center offering a peaceful walking path, popular for short strolls.

sites.name: Eco Park
sites.description: A clean, eco-friendly recreational park featuring water features, a small zoo, and a museum, blending African and Western design.

sites.name: National Museum of Yaoundé
sites.description: Housed in a former presidential palace, this museum showcases Cameroon's history, art, and cultural heritage.
